---
title: "readme"
format: 
  html: default
  markdown_github: default
editor: visual
---

# Project description

This project investigates the metabolomic profile of pediatric patients with Juvenile Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (JSLE) in comparison with patients with Juvenile Idiopathic Arthritis (JIA) and healthy controls, using ^1^H nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) spectroscopy data. The primary objective is to evaluate whether the SLEDAI-2K (Systemic Lupus Erythematosus Disease Activity Index 2000) disease activity index correlates with alterations in the serum metabolome of patients, as well as to assess whether the active and remission phases of the disease can be distinguished based on metabolic profiles.

Multivariate analyses — including Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and Partial Least Squares Discriminant Analysis (PLS-DA) — demonstrate that the JSLE and JIA groups differ from the control group. However, the separation among JSLE activity subgroups (remission, mild, and severe, as classified by SLEDAI-2K) is limited. Among all metabolites evaluated, only the **VLDL/LDL** signal showed a statistically significant correlation with the SLEDAI-2K score (r = 0.41; p = 0.033), suggesting that alterations in the lipid profile may reflect the inflammatory activity of the disease.

The complete results of the analysis are available in the file [**Lupus_SLEDAI.pdf**](Lupus_SLEDAI.pdf).

# Project file description

The project is organized as follows:

```
LESJ_SLEDAI_metabolomics_public/
│
├── inputs/                                    # Input data
│   ├── Metabolomica_plus_dados_gerais.xlsx    # Metabolomic (NMR) data integrated with clinical and demographic patient variables
│   └── Sample Metadata.xlsx                   # Sample metadata (identification, group, collection information)
│
├── Lupus_SLEDAI.qmd                           # Quarto document containing all R code and analysis narrative
├── Lupus_SLEDAI.pdf                           # PDF report with analysis results (primary output)
├── Lupus_SLEDAI.html                          # Interactive HTML version of the analysis report
├── readme.qmd                                 # This file — general project description
└── LESJ_SLEDAI_public.Rproj                   # RStudio project file
```

The analysis code in `Lupus_SLEDAI.qmd` performs the following steps: (1) median normalization of metabolomic data with Pareto scaling; (2) multivariate PCA and PLS-DA analyses for group discrimination; (3) classification of JSLE patients into activity subgroups (remission, mild, and severe) based on SLEDAI-2K; (4) univariate tests (Kruskal-Wallis and Mann-Whitney) and correlation analysis between metabolites and the SLEDAI-2K score; and (5) visualizations including hierarchical heatmap and correlation plots.

# Sample information

The dataset includes serum samples from three groups: JSLE patients, JIA patients, and healthy controls. JSLE patients underwent SLEDAI-2K scoring, collected within the 10 days prior to sample collection, enabling classification into remission (SLEDAI-2K = 0), mild (0 < SLEDAI-2K ≤ 6), and severe (SLEDAI-2K > 6). All JSLE patients were receiving hydroxychloroquine, with some receiving additional immunosuppressants, which represents a potential confounding variable in the interpretation of metabolic profiles. The number of patients in remission is limited compared to those in the active phase, reducing the statistical power for comparisons among activity subgroups.
